level_signals: 3+ years stakeholder management or customer-facing role (hard floor)
Nice-to-have signals 10+ years of professional experience
Comp range $187K–$234K base in DC, consistent with senior IC level
Leads cross-functional teams and drives organizational change

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of experience succeeding in stakeholder management or customer-facing role
A technical background (education or professional experience with CS, Economics, Statistics, Engineering)
A deep understanding of ML operations process
A track record of structured, analytics-driven problem-solving
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Willingness to travel at least 25% of the time

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of professional experience, often in a customer-facing technical program management role in industry or government.
Prior experience at an API technology company and / or managing technical customers using an API
Proficiency in Python, SQL or other programming languages

scope.surface: Public Sector Delivery — AI/ML solutions and products for U.S. government and public sector customers (computer vision and generative AI domains)
scope.business_model: B2G (business-to-government) / enterprise AI data and full-stack technology platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer retention and long-term partnership health
Repeatability and scalability of customer success processes
Issue resolution rate and triage efficiency
core_responsibilities: Drive innovation and transformation by diving deep into technical and operational challenges, solving undefined problems, and delivering impactful solutions for Public Sector customers
Leverage technical concepts, tools, and expertise to create solutions from scratch or drastically improve existing solutions
Use data, analytics, and technical expertise to align internal resources and create tools that directly add value to customers in the public sector space
Transition AI/ML technologies and processes into working Public Sector products / solutions even when requirements are undefined or ambiguous
Partner with engineering, operations, and other public sector teams to build and deliver AI systems tailored to unique government use cases in the computer vision and generative AI domains
Lead a cross-functional team to exceed the customer's AI/ML objectives
Manage the long-term health of the customer base by identifying and preempting areas of risk or concern
Strategically identify ways we can make customer success repeatable and solve issues for future customers
Address engineering problems alongside the product development teams

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($187K–$234K base in Washington DC)
Clear must-have list including specific clearance requirement — reduces ambiguity in screening
Comprehensive benefits package described including health, dental, vision, retirement, L&D stipend, and generous PTO
red_flags: Active TS/SCI clearance is a hard requirement — eliminates the vast majority of candidates and cannot be quickly obtained
Role appears to require onsite presence in Washington DC area given clearance-sensitive work, but location type is not explicitly stated as remote/hybrid/onsite in the body
3+ year floor is low for the seniority implied by comp and responsibilities; the 10+ year nice-to-have signals actual expectations may be much higher
90-day waiting period policy before reconsidering candidates for same role noted explicitly

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, responsibilities, and 10+ year nice-to-have signal)
location.type (inferred as onsite based on TS/SCI clearance requirement and Washington DC salary basis; not explicitly stated in body)
location.work_auth (inferred as us-citizenship-required due to active TS/SCI clearance hard requirement)
location.visa_sponsorship (inferred as no due to TS/SCI clearance requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer examples)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: The JD does not explicitly state whether the role is onsite, hybrid, or remote. However, an active TS/SCI clearance and Washington DC salary range strongly imply onsite or hybrid with DC presence. Treated as onsite here.
Must-have floor is '3+ years' but nice-to-have signals '10+ years'; true experience expectation is ambiguous — could screen for either depending on recruiter.
The nice-to-haves section is framed as 'many have' rather than 'preferred' or 'bonus' — treated as soft/nice-to-have here but the language is slightly unusual.
